channel.title: Global Sanctions News (Custom RSS)
channel.description: Auto-generated feed from rendered Global Sanctions news list

item.title: UN lists Congo River Alliance military group and paramilitary leaders
item.description: The Resolution 1533 (2004) UN Security Council Sanctions Committee has added 6 people and 2 entities to its DRC sanctions list. Those designated are: Alliance Fleuve Congo (a.k.a. the Congo River

item.title: European Court of Justice rejects Timchenkos’ delisting appeals
item.description: The Court of Justice of the EU has rejected appeals brought by Gennady and Elena Timchenko against General Court judgments rejecting challenges to their relistings under Regulation (EU) 269/2014. See

item.title: EU delays lowering Russia oil price cap under Reg 833
item.description: The EU has amended Regulation (EU) 833/2014 to delay lowering the price cap on Russian oil until 23 July 2026. The oil price cap was introduced in 2022. It is currently USD 44.10/barrel. Article 3n Re

item.title: EU rejects Russian businessman’s fourth delisting application
item.description: The EU General Court has rejected a Russian-Cypriot businessman’s challenge to his September 2024, March 2025 and September 2025 sanctions relistings under Regulation (EU) 269/2014 – OT v Council, Cas

item.title: US adds 7 IRGC-linked people & entities to non-proliferation sanctions list
item.description: The US has added 4 people & 3 entities to its EO 13882 non-proliferation sanctions list – press release. See our US chemical weapons & non-proliferation page for the EO 13382 designation crite

item.title: UK makes first state threat designations – lists IRGC, IMCR and GRU volunteer corps
item.description: The UK has made its first foreign state threat designations made under the National Security Act 2023. The National Security Act (Designated Bodies) Regulations 2026 lists: The Islamic Revolution

item.title: European court rejects Dana Astra’s Belarus sanctions delisting appeal but finds it was brought in time
item.description: Case C-363/25 P – Dana Astra v Council: The Court of Justice of the EU has dismissed an appeal by Belarusian company Dana Astra against General Court judgment Dana Astra v Council (T-281/23), which re

item.title: Canadian court orders shareholder buyout pending GAC determination of ownership & control
item.description: The Ontario Superior Court has held that a court-ordered share buyout should not take place until Global Affairs Canada (GAC), Canada's sanctions regulator, decides whether the selling shareholder is

item.title: UK adds 4 people and 7 entities to Sudan Sanctions list for participating in illicit gold and finance networks
item.description: The UK has added 4 people and 7 entities to the Sudan sanctions list under the Sudan (Sanctions) (EU Exit) Regulations 2020 – press release. The designations are said to target the conflict gold trade

item.title: EU General Court rejects claims brought by Lithuanian company against prohibitions on the transfer of potash products from Belarus through the EU
item.description: T‑261/24, Birių Krovinių Terminalas v Council – The General Court of the EU has rejected a series of claims brought by Lithuanian company Birių Krovinių Terminalas (“BKT”). BKT (a transit operator tha
